# UC-10.16.12 · Microsoft Defender Attack Surface Reduction (ASR) Rule Triggers from Event Hub

## Description

ASR events streamed through Defender advanced hunting into Splunk highlight policy enforcement gaps, noisy rules worth tuning, and hosts repeatedly violating block or audit rules.

## Value

Operationalizes ASR beyond the Defender portal by correlating rule triggers with asset ownership and change tickets, and by spotting concentrated abuse on a small host population.

## Implementation

Configure the Event Hub modular input for Microsoft Defender advanced hunting per add-on documentation. Ensure ActionType is extracted; some builds nest fields under JSON—use `spath` if needed. Create lookups for approved ASR exclusions and subtract known-good paths. Start in audit mode baselines before alerting on block noise.

## SPL

```spl
index=* sourcetype="ms:defender:eventhub" earliest=-24h
| eval action=coalesce(ActionType, action_type, "")
| where like(action, "%Asr%") OR like(lower(action), "%attack surface reduction%")
| eval device=coalesce(DeviceName, deviceName, host, dest)
| stats count by action, device
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (top ASR ActionType values), table (device × rule), timeline of trigger volume.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6207)
